content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks that rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback can be augmented by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives central to modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hybrid self-improvement workflow that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision while resolving its common limitation of getting stuck in local feedback loops. This hybrid framework would draw on conflicting perspective checks from self-reflection theory to break the stagnation that occurs when a single LLM cannot identify flaws in its own refined outputs, without sacrificing the simplicity that makes Self-Refine appealing for edge deployments.
